title: 8 Herbal Essential Oils For Palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great help for palpitations, which are irregular heartbeats that can be caused by stress, anxiety, or other factors. Some herbs have a calming effect on the body, which can help slow down the heart rate and reduce palpitations. For instance, Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. This can be especially helpful for people who experience palpitations due to stress or anxiety.  Another herb that can be beneficial for palpitations is Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower. This herb has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and insomnia, and its calming effect can help reduce palpitations. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, passionflower can help the heart rate return to a normal rhythm.  Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, is another herb that can help with palpitations. Its calming scent can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to palpitations. When inhaled, the essential oil of lavender can slow down the heart rate and promote relaxation, making it a great natural remedy for palpitations.  Herbal teas made from these herbs can be a great way to reap their benefits. Drinking a warm cup of valerian root tea, for example, can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Similarly, a cup of passionflower tea can help reduce anxiety and promote a restful night's sleep. And a cup of lavender tea can be a great way to unwind and reduce stress.  By incorporating herbal essential oils and teas into your daily routine, you can experience a range of benefits, including reduced anxiety, improved sleep, and a slower heart rate. This can be especially helpful for people who experience palpitations due to stress or anxiety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety that can cause palpitations. Valeriana officinalis essential oils also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to slow down a rapid heartbeat. The sedative properties of valerenic acid can help to regulate the heart rate and reduce palpitations. By reducing anxiety and stress, and regulating the heart rate, Valeriana officinalis essential oils can help to alleviate palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba oil) in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 tablespoons of Valeriana officinalis roots to a clean glass jar and pour in 1 cup of carrier oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops as needed to help manage palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are active constituents that contribute to its therapeutic properties. The flavonoids, including vitexin and isovitexin, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the heart rate and reduce palpitations. The alkaloids, such as harmine and harmaline, have a relaxing effect on the body, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations. The glycosides, including passiflorine, also have a sedative effect, further helping to calm the heart and reduce palpitations. By combining these properties, Passiflora incarnata essential oils can help regulate heart rhythm and alleviate palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Use a blender to crush the leaves and flowers into a fine paste. Use 1 tablespoon of water if needed.
Transfer the paste into a steam distillation apparatus or a double boiler.
Steam distill the mixture for 2-3 hours. Collect the essential oil that separates from the water.
Strain the collected oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle. Label and store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have a calming and relaxing effect on the body, which can help reduce palpitations. The sedative properties of linalool and linalyl acetate help slow down the heart rate and promote a sense of calmness. This, in turn, can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related palpitations. The anxiolytic and anti-arrhythmic properties of these compounds can also help regulate heart rhythm and reduce palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of water in a large bowl.
Steep the flowers in the water for 1 hour, then strain the mixture using a cheesecloth.
Transfer the flower extract to a saucepan and simmer on low heat for 30 minutes.
Collect the essential oil by placing a paper towel in a strainer and letting it drip into a cup, then discard the paper.
Transfer the collected essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains a number of bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. These properties include its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to palpitations. Melissa officinalis essential oils also has a sedative effect, helping to regulate heart rate and promote a sense of calm. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate physical symptoms associated with palpitations. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis essential oils can help mitigate the causes of palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil like jojoba oil or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Add the fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers to the jar and ensure they are fully covered by the carrier oil.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils contains boswellic acids, ferulic acid, and other bioactive constituents that help with palpitations. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce stress and anxiety that can cause irregular heartbeats. The ferulic acid in Avena sativa essential oils has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to regulate heart rate and blood pressure. The anti-inflammatory properties of boswellic acids also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. By reducing stress and inflammation, Avena sativa essential oils can help to calm palpitations and promote a healthy heart rhythm.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa seeds and 4 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Dry the Avena sativa seeds in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind 1 cup of dried Avena sativa seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 4 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well and seal the jar.
Leave the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, then strain and discard the solids to get the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. The oil also contains terpenes and sesquiterpenes, which have vasodilatory effects, meaning they help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow. This increased blood flow can help regulate heart rate and reduce palpitations. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots, 1 cup of water, and a glass jar with a lid.
Peel the ginger roots and cut them into small pieces. Place them in the glass jar.
Pour the cup of water over the ginger pieces, then cover the jar with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 2-3 days, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains bioactive constituents such as bilobalide, ginkgolides, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce palpitations by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba essential oils have a calming effect on the heart, which can help slow down rapid heartbeats. The terpenoids, including bilobalide, may also help regulate heart rhythm and reduce anxiety-related palpitations. The combined effects of these properties can help alleviate palpitations and promote a more stable heart rhythm.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of Ginkgo biloba leaves. Use fresh leaves for best results.
Combine 250g of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This helps release the oil from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops of the oil per use, as needed for palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oils contains apigenin and luteolin, two bioactive constituents that contribute to its calming effects. These compounds help to soothe the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to palpitations. The anxiolytic properties of apigenin and luteolin also help to slow down heart rate and promote relaxation. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla essential oils contains bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce stress and promote a sense of calm. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, Matricaria chamomilla essential oils can help to alleviate palpitations caused by stress and anxiety.
remedies.image_alt: Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oils for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Matricaria chamomilla flowers, 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba oil), and a dark glass bottle with a lid.
Combine the Matricaria chamomilla flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Store the bottle in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. This allows the oil to mature and become more potent.
After 2 weeks, check the oil's color and consistency. If it's ready, use it as needed. You can store it in the fridge to prolong its shelf life.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce palpitations. Some herbs, like passionflower and lavender, have a calming effect on the body. They can help slow down a rapid heartbeat and promote relaxation. This can be especially helpful for people who experience stress-related palpitations. The soothing properties of herbal tea may help alleviate symptoms naturally.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help calm palpitations, it's essential to be cautious about consuming them daily. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can interact with medications or worsen certain heart conditions. If you're sensitive, you might experience an allergic reaction or stomach upset. Monitor your body's response and adjust your consumption accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with palpitations in a few weeks. Some people notice a difference within a week or two, while it may take longer for others. It's essential to be patient and consistent with your herbal tea consumption. Regular intake can help calm the heart and reduce palpitations over time.
faq_time: If you experience palpitations, a soothing cup of herbal tea might help calm your heart. Drinking it in the evening, about an hour before bed, can be beneficial. As you relax before sleep, the tea's calming effects can help ease palpitations, promoting a more peaceful night's rest and a quieter heart.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for palpitation. For example, ginseng tea might increase the risk of irregular heartbeat when taken with certain heart medications. Similarly, peppermint tea could lower blood pressure, which might be problematic if you're already taking blood pressure medication. Be cautious when drinking herbal teas with your prescribed medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have palpitations. Some herbal teas, like those with caffeine or certain herbs like ginseng, may not be good for you or your baby. If you still want to try herbal teas, look for ones that are known to be safe during pregnancy, like peppermint or chamomile.
faq_breastfeeding: When you're breastfeeding and experiencing palpitations, it's a good idea to check the ingredients of herbal teas you consume. Some herbal teas, like those containing foxglove or digitalis, can interact with medications or worsen palpitations. Look for teas made from herbs like peppermint, chamomile, or lavender, which are generally considered safe.
